author: Johnathan Ritzi
description: Extract text from attachments and store it as Markdown in your notes.

manifest.author: Johnathan Ritzi
manifest.description: Extract text from PDFs, documents, images, etc. and store it as Markdown in your notes.
manifest.name: OCR Extractor

name: OCR Extractor

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changed
* Security and stability improvements

## Fixed
* Fixed "Invalid page request" error that could occur when extracting text from multiple PDFs concurrently (#109)
* Password-protected and unreadable PDFs are now skipped instead of aborting the extraction (#109)
* Fixed Tesseract engine race conditions that could occur when extracting multiple attachments at once
* Changing a setting while an extraction is running no longer affects the in-progress run

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.4.0...2.4.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added
- A [public API](https://www.npmjs.com/package/ocr-extractor-api) that other plugins can use to extract text from attachments via the user's configured OCR engine

## Changed
- Updated the Examples section with a Chinese book excerpt (non-Latin script)
- Updated the README to reflect the release of Mistral OCR 4

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.3.2...2.4.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added
* Added Arabic translation (https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/discussions/89)

## Changed
* Renamed "OCR service" to "OCR engine" (no change in behavior)
* Security and stability improvements

## Fixed
* WebP images now extract successfully (https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/discussions/62#discussioncomment-17312198)
* Custom commands respect your shell's PATH, so you no longer need to specify an absolute path
* Fixed license generation to avoid Obsidian Community "Build output does not match" warning

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.3.1...2.3.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed
* Updates for better compatibility with Obsidian Community scanner

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.3.0...2.3.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added
* An OpenAI-compatible API service (with model picker and Test button), to easily extract text with local or cloud models
* Support for extracting text in all notes in a given folder (via a right-click menu option and a command)
* Support for extracting text in selected notes (via a right-click menu option)
* Support for extracting text in a single note (via a right-click menu option)

## Changed
* Renamed the "Use embedded PDF text" setting to clarify that OCR is used as a fallback if there is no embedded text
* Improved image handling for better OCR output
* Improved error handling and messaging
* Reworded terminology to match the Obsidian style guide
* Improved documentation, including Obsidian Publish examples with different services and files
* Security and stability improvements

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.2.2...2.3.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added
* Added a modal prompting the user to update their Obsidian installer version when it's below the minimum supported by the plugin (fixes https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/issues/57)

## Changed
* Developer improvements (E2E tests run against different combinations of installer and app versions)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.2.1...2.2.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changed
* Make cancellation of in-progress extraction more robust
* Improve retry handling for Mistral errors
* Security hardening
* Dependency upgrades and developer improvements

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.2.0...2.2.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added
* Added a "Cancel extraction" command

## Changed
* Dependency upgrades and developer improvements (improved E2E test suite)

## Fixed
* Fixed attachments being skipped (with a "moved during processing" warning) when using Markdown link embed syntax (#51)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.1.0...2.2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Added
* Added a setting to automatically extract text when a new attachment is added to a note

## Changed
* Added logic to prevent attempting to extract text from Obsidian-native file types (Markdown, Canvas, Base)
* Added the plugin name to the beginning of some notices, to make it clear what plugin they're related to
* Dependency upgrades and developer improvements

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.0.1...2.1.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed
* Corrected the 2.0.0 release, which was built from the wrong commit

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/2.0.0...2.0.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Breaking changes
* Updated the "Extracted text" callouts to use a new custom type `[!ocr-extractor]` instead of `[!summary]`, to allow those callouts to be more robustly managed by the plugin (and use a custom icon). Both callout formats will continue to be supported, and extracting text (for a given note or all notes) will update callouts to the new version. But this is technically a breaking change that would affect anyone relying on the current exact format of those headers.

## Added
* Added a setting to use text embedded in a PDF if available, rather than extracting with OCR
* Localized the plugin to support multiple languages (starting with Latin American Spanish, French, and Simplified Chinese). The "Extracted text" callout header will be updated to the current language whenever extracting text in a given note.

## Changed
* Updated Mistral settings to use Obsidian's [Secret Storage](https://docs.obsidian.md/plugins/guides/secret-storage) instead of a text field to securely store the API key (automatically migrated when upgrading)
* Changed the custom command "Test" button to be able to distinguish between 4 cases: success, wrong text, no output file, or error. It now tests using an image with the text "OCR test" instead of a 1x1 image.
* Removed the "Extract text in all notes" option on mobile, which isn't supported and was previously always present but disabled
* Simplified UI text
* Developer improvements (switched to pnpm, added a CI workflow, improved eslint config, ensure dependency licenses are added to the final bundle)

## Fixed
* When a custom command does not produce an output file (e.g. for unsupported attachment types), it will skip the attachment instead of causing an error

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/1.3.0...2.0.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
* Add support for custom commands
* Simplify modal wording
* Bump lodash-es from 4.17.22 to 4.17.23 by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/pull/6

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
* Add support for local OCR with Tesseract
* Improved architecture for adding other services in the future
* Better handling of Mistral errors
* Improve status handling and messaging
* Improve OCR service documentation

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/1.1.0...1.2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
* Add mobile support
* Remove trailing whitespace in extracted text
* More robust handling of blank lines before and after the inserted callout
* Use modal instead of status bar for single note extraction
* Use consistent line endings regardless of platform
* Show notice when extraction canceled
* Minor bug fixes and dependency upgrades

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/1.0.1...1.1.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
* Skip processing files with unsupported MIME type
* Use Obsidian helpers to build description for setting tab
* More robust request handling with retries, batch requests, and better cancellation behavior
* Fix bug with multiple embeds on the same line
* Minor cleanup

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jritzi/ocr-extractor/compare/1.0.0...1.0.1
